projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

insights.summary: The system is experiencing multiple critical camera outages (front_yard and dog_run) precisely during a period of low human activity, suggesting a significant loss of situational awareness. This technical failure is highly concerning and requires immediate maintenance attention to ensure continuous monitoring coverage, especially since the family is currently away from the main monitored areas.
[front_yard] The camera stream is down, and audio indicates a child is crying in distress at the front door.
[front_yard] The camera stream has failed, showing only an error message after the first frame.

The combination of the doorbell ringing, the dogs barking across multiple locations, and the ongoing background audio (TV/music) at 11:44 suggests an immediate, external event, likely a visitor or delivery person, despite all cameras reporting empty areas. This requires confirmation of who is at the door.
[front_yard] Dogs are barking urgently at the front yard, but no dogs are visible in the current view.

The combination of the high-priority smoke/CO alarm event at 10:11, the dogs' persistent and multi-point barking, and the current presence signal (10 mobile devices home) suggests the household is dealing with the aftermath of a potential hazard or ongoing stress. The unclassified audio across multiple zones (entryway, backyard, dog_run) indicates residual activity that needs investigation, despite the cameras reporting 'empty' areas.
[dog_run] The 'dog_run' camera is offline and not receiving video frames.
[entryway] A smoke or carbon monoxide alarm is beeping at the front door, which is a high-priority safety concern.
[dog_run] The 'dog_run' camera is offline, preventing monitoring of the dog run area.

There is a high-stress pattern involving the dogs' persistent, multi-point barking and multiple camera failures (specifically the 'dog_run' camera failing twice). Crucially, the family members (Steve, Miller, Penelope) are not visible in the last hour, following a recorded incident of a child crying in distress at 09:07 AM. This suggests the family may be absent or preoccupied following a significant, unresolved event.
